title: Image Classification in Depth Capstone
concept: Phase 6 Capstone: We review single-label, multi-label, fine-grained, hierarchical, calibration, evaluation, and zero-shot paradigms.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main benefit of custom prompt templates in zero-shot vision tasks?

options.text: They align text inputs with the pre-trained caption distribution of the model.
They reduce the computational depth of the text transformer.
They replace the need for an image encoder.

exercises.explain: Correct! Templates match the descriptive syntax of the web dataset that the model learned during pretraining.

cards.title: The complete classification map
cards.body: You started this phase looking at basic dog vs. cat problems. Over these 7 units, you have transformed into a classification architect, capable of mapping nested concepts, evaluating multi-tag images, and building zero-shot matchers.

cards.title: Phase 6 Summary
cards.body:
We have mastered:
- **Single vs. Multi-label**: Softmax + CE vs. Sigmoid + BCE.
- **Fine-grained**: High intra-class variance, attention models, and bilinear pooling.
- **Hierarchical**: Tree vs. DAG taxonomies, distance losses, and probability chains.
- **Calibration**: Platt/Temperature scaling and ECE.
- **Evaluation**: Precision/Recall trade-offs, confusion matrices, ROC/PR curves.
- **Few/Zero-shot**: Siamese metric learning, CLIP, and prompt engineering.

cards.title: A toolkit for any lock
cards.body: Imagine you are a locksmith. Simple classification is having one basic key (ResNet). This phase has given you a complete lock-picking set: picks for complex double locks (multi-label), magnifier lenses for microscopic pins (fine-grained), skeleton keys for master door trees (hierarchical), and digital keys that adjust automatically (calibration/zero-shot).

cards.title: Designing a complex visual pipeline
cards.body: When building an medical assistant system: you use multi-label classification to identify concurrent pathologies, hierarchical routing to fallback safely when rare conditions appear, temperature scaling to ensure calibrated risk scores, and PR curves to evaluate rare conditions correctly.

cards.title: ML is more than just raw backbone accuracy
cards.body: It's a myth that ML engineering is only about tweaking layers or building deeper models. In the real world, understanding the label structure (hierarchies), managing certainty (calibration), choosing correct business metrics, and leveraging foundation embeddings shape system value far more than the exact backbone choice.

cards.title: Classification Architecture Taxonomy
cards.body: Selecting the right tool for the classification task:

right.text:
Calibration scaling (ECE / Temperature)
Evaluation metrics (Precision, Recall, PR Curves)
Few/Zero-Shot Adapters (Siamese / CLIP)

exercises.prompt: Which combination of activation and loss is the standard for training multi-label classification networks?
exercises.explain: Multi-label classification treats each class as an independent binary decision, which requires Sigmoid activations and BCE loss.

options.text: Sigmoid activation on output nodes with Binary Cross-Entropy (BCE) Loss
Softmax activation with standard Cross-Entropy Loss
ReLU activation with Mean Squared Error
Sigmoid activation with standard Cross-Entropy Loss

exercises.prompt: True or false: Applying Temperature Scaling changes a model's predicted categories and lowers its overall accuracy.
exercises.explain: False. Temperature scaling divides logits by a constant T, which preserves their relative order, leaving predicted categories and accuracy completely unchanged.
A shared embedding space allows image and text vectors to be compared directly using distance metrics.
exercises.sentence: CLIP maps images and text captions into a shared ___ space to match corresponding pairs.

exercises.prompt: Which metric should be prioritized when the business cost of a missed positive detection (False Negative) is extremely high?
exercises.explain: Recall measures the fraction of actual positive cases detected, so prioritizing recall minimizes missed cases.

exercises.prompt: We have completed our study of advanced classification paradigms. What is the next logical step in our computer vision learning path?

options.text: Move from whole-image classification to localizing objects with Object Detection Foundations.
Go back and retrain LeNet on MNIST again.
Switch to studying natural language text generation.
Delete all networks and build classical filters only.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Now that we can classify images in complex, structured, and data-efficient ways, the next step is to locate multiple objects inside a single image. Phase 6: Image Classification in Depth is complete. Next up: Object Detection Foundations.
